metadata.title:
GOVERNMENT REFORM


NOW UP TO THE PEOPLE

pages.raw_text:
The city council had its inning
last night when it passed all the
amendments proposed by the char
ter revision committee on the
ground that 1t was for the people to
decide whether they wanted the
amendment or not.


In the next breath the council
proposed an amendment of its own,
President Lambert submitting the
amendment relating to the board of
public works and excluding the en-.
gineer from the board. The amend
ment also gives the mayor power to
appoint and remove city officials
without confirmatio NOT the council.
This brought Councilmen Ostrander
and Baldwin to their feet in pro-
test. They pointed out the muddle
which might result from the pas
sage of both amendments, but the
majority of the council stood pat
and insisted in the words of the
charter revision committee that it
was up to the people to decide
which one they preferred. Mr..
Baldwin did not talk q great deal
